sections.content:
Now I see the changes clearly:
Before:
- Node 13: White (W) 100 pixels, boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - So it was a 10x10 white square at rows 34-43, cols 27-36
After:
- Node 13: Purple (p) 50 pixels, boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [38, 36], [38, 27]] - A 5x10 purple rectangle at rows 34-38, cols 27-36
- Node 14: White (W) 50 pixels, boundary [[39, 27], [39, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]] - A 5x10 white rectangle at rows 39-43, cols 27-36
So the white square split into purple top half and white bottom half.
So SPACE caused the white square to split the white square into purple on top, white on bottom. This matches the pattern in the top-left corner where white is on top and purple is on bottom.
This looks like the pattern from the top-left corner: white rectangle above purple rectangle. So SPACE seems to transform the white square to match that pattern.
But wait - the center has a purple rectangle above a white square. After SPACE, the white square became purple-on-top, white-on-bottom. So it seems like SPACE is transforming objects.
Let me think about the game might be about matching patterns. The top-left shows white above purple. The center shows purple above white. After SPACE, the white below. Maybe I need to match them.
Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it cycles or reverses.
